assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines 'available' as the latest stable version that can be manually upgraded to right now, which matches the expectation of versions available for manual upgrade

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines 'default' as what new clusters get but incorrectly states it's typically newer than available and doesn't mention the relationship with auto-upgrade target or separate promotion stages

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies auto-upgrade target as what clusters will be upgraded to during automatic maintenance and mentions it's cluster-specific

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explain the relationship between default and auto-upgrade target versions or mention they can be the same or differ during minor version rollouts

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response mentions auto-upgrade target is cluster-specific but doesn't discuss maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions, or that different clusters on same channel can have different targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention maintenance exclusions or how they affect auto-upgrade targets, particularly 'no minor' exclusions

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the specific gcloud command for checking upgrade info, target versions, or EoS timestamps

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response includes a link to GKE release notes and recommends checking them for known issues
